---
title: "California Map with Wind Turbines"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(mapdata)
library(ggplot2)

# Load the map data for U.S. states
us_states <- map_data("state")

# Filter out data for California
california <- subset(us_states, region == "california")

# Print the first few rows of the data to check the structure
head(california)

# Load the wind turbine data
load("C:/Users/Lenovo/Downloads/wind_turbines.rda")  # Updated path

# Check the structure of the wind_turbines dataset
str(wind_turbines)

# Filter wind turbines located in California
wind_ca <- subset(wind_turbines, t_state == "CA")

# Check if the filtering works
head(wind_ca)

# Plot the map of California using ggplot and geom_polygon
ggplot(data = california, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.1) +  # Maintain aspect ratio for proper map display
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Map of California")

# Plot the map with wind turbine locations using geom_point()
ggplot(data = california, aes(x = long, y = lat)) +
  geom_polygon(fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +  # State boundaries
  geom_point(data = wind_ca, aes(x = xlong, y = ylat), color = "red", size = 2) +  # Wind turbine locations
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.1) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "California Map with Wind Turbine Locations")
```
